findings.message: All devDependencies use ^ (caret) ranges allowing minor/patch version drift on reinstall, which could pull in undiscovered vulnerabilities.
All production dependencies use ^ ranges (e.g. commander ^13.1.0, open ^10.1.0) allowing minor version drift in CLI distribution.
plugin.json has no `version` field; R48 marks version as recommended and R50 requires it to be bumped in four places on every release.
MCP server configured as external HTTP endpoint (https://mcp.context7.com/mcp); all tool calls route over the network to a third-party service.
Agent has zero <example> blocks; R09 requires minimum 2 with Context + user message + assistant response for reliable triggering.
Agent body lists response content (direct answer, code examples, links) but provides no formal output template; response structure will vary between invocations.
Five vague quantifiers: 'concise' and 'actionable' (L11), 'focused' (L5), 'relevant' (L26), 'appropriate' (L24).
Command frontmatter is missing the required `name` field; command discoverability and registration may fail in strict plugin systems.
Command calls MCP tools `resolve-library-id` and `query-docs` but declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter field; hosts enforcing tool allowlists will reject the calls.
Command takes a required <library> argument but specifies no behavior when $ARGUMENTS is blank — no error message or default behavior defined.
Command states 'Results include code examples and explanations' but defines no structured output template; format varies between invocations.
Two vague quantifiers: 'best' and 'relevant' both on L33 ('finds the best matching library', 'documentation relevant to your query').
Three vague quantifiers: 'better' (L30 and L55) and 'closest' (L39).

skills/context7-mcp/SKILL.md is byte-for-byte identical across plugins/claude/context7/skills/, plugins/cursor/context7/skills/, and skills/ — any fix requires three identical edits, making drift likely over time.
Agent has zero <example> blocks; same issue as the Claude plugin variant.
Same missing output template as the Claude plugin variant.
Agent frontmatter has no `model` field; the equivalent Claude plugin agent declares `model: sonnet`, indicating this was an oversight during copy.
Same five vague quantifiers as the Claude plugin agent (identical content): 'concise', 'actionable', 'focused', 'relevant', 'appropriate'.
Cursor agent is a copy of the Claude agent but is missing the `model: sonnet` frontmatter field present in the original, the first visible sign of copy-paste drift between plugin variants.
MCP server configured as external HTTP OAuth endpoint (https://mcp.context7.com/mcp/oauth); same external data-routing concern as the Claude plugin variant.
Same three vague quantifiers as the Claude plugin SKILL.md (identical content): 'better' (L30, L55), 'closest' (L39).
Two vague quantifiers: 'best' (L50, 'use the best result you have') and 'closest' (L101, 'Use the closest match to what the user specified').
Same three vague quantifiers as the two plugin SKILL.md copies (identical content): 'better' (L30, L55), 'closest' (L39).
Four vague quantifiers: 'best' (L50), 'most relevant match' (L80), 'closest match' (L101), 'relevant details' (L116).
Command frontmatter is missing the required `name` field; command discoverability and registration may fail in strict plugin systems.
Command calls MCP tools `resolve-library-id` and `query-docs` but declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter field; hosts enforcing tool allowlists will reject the calls.
MCP server configured as external HTTP endpoint (https://mcp.context7.com/mcp); all tool calls route over the network to a third-party service.
MCP server configured as external HTTP OAuth endpoint (https://mcp.context7.com/mcp/oauth); same external data-routing concern as the Claude plugin variant.
All devDependencies use ^ (caret) ranges allowing minor/patch version drift on reinstall, which could pull in undiscovered vulnerabilities.
All production dependencies use ^ ranges (e.g. commander ^13.1.0, open ^10.1.0) allowing minor version drift in CLI distribution.
Agent has zero <example> blocks; R09 requires minimum 2 with Context + user message + assistant response for reliable triggering.
Agent has zero <example> blocks; same issue as the Claude plugin variant.
Agent body lists response content (direct answer, code examples, links) but provides no formal output template; response structure will vary between invocations.
Same missing output template as the Claude plugin variant.
Agent frontmatter has no `model` field; the equivalent Claude plugin agent declares `model: sonnet`, indicating this was an oversight during copy.
Command takes a required <library> argument but specifies no behavior when $ARGUMENTS is blank — no error message or default behavior defined.
Command states 'Results include code examples and explanations' but defines no structured output template; format varies between invocations.
Five vague quantifiers: 'concise' and 'actionable' (L11), 'focused' (L5), 'relevant' (L26), 'appropriate' (L24).
Same five vague quantifiers as the Claude plugin agent (identical content): 'concise', 'actionable', 'focused', 'relevant', 'appropriate'.
Two vague quantifiers: 'best' and 'relevant' both on L33 ('finds the best matching library', 'documentation relevant to your query').
Three vague quantifiers: 'better' (L30 and L55) and 'closest' (L39).
Same three vague quantifiers as the Claude plugin SKILL.md (identical content): 'better' (L30, L55), 'closest' (L39).
Same three vague quantifiers as the two plugin SKILL.md copies (identical content): 'better' (L30, L55), 'closest' (L39).
Two vague quantifiers: 'best' (L50, 'use the best result you have') and 'closest' (L101, 'Use the closest match to what the user specified').
Four vague quantifiers: 'best' (L50), 'most relevant match' (L80), 'closest match' (L101), 'relevant details' (L116).
plugin.json has no `version` field; R48 marks version as recommended and R50 requires it to be bumped in four places on every release.

skills/context7-mcp/SKILL.md is byte-for-byte identical across plugins/claude/context7/skills/, plugins/cursor/context7/skills/, and skills/ — any fix requires three identical edits, making drift likely over time.
Cursor agent is a copy of the Claude agent but is missing the `model: sonnet` frontmatter field present in the original, the first visible sign of copy-paste drift between plugin variants.
